sprint.name: Forge Expansion Sprint
sprint.note: A scheduled review is not a guaranteed release. The forge publishes at most one coherent change when it clears the quality and safety gates; otherwise it skips the window.
sprint.secondaryAutomation: A separate GitHub Actions workflow runs Tuesday and Friday at 15:17 UTC. It can only create or improve approved declarative-engine apps and records accepted publishes in this ledger.

method.label: Inspect
method.description: Review the live site, repository, current apps, open feedback, bugs, depth, accessibility, and portfolio gaps.

method.label: Compare
method.description: Consider at least three candidate changes and choose the one that creates the strongest useful depth or breadth.

method.label: Build one
method.description: Create or improve one coherent feature or app, including mobile, keyboard, touch, reduced-motion, teardown, and edge cases.

method.label: Validate
method.description: Run JavaScript syntax checks, feedback-routing tests, registry rules, required-file checks, and safety-boundary validation.

method.label: Publish or skip
method.description: Merge and deploy only after every required check passes. Skip when the candidate is weak, unsafe, duplicative, or incomplete.

qualityGates: One coherent change maximum per review window
No secrets, trackers, accounts, hidden storage, or unreviewed network behavior
No direct execution of user feedback
Mobile, keyboard, touch, focus, and reduced-motion behavior considered
Repository validation must pass before main is published

queue.title: Relevance Lab 1.1 strategy regression playtest
queue.reason: Verify scenario-specific signal sensitivities, ranking movement, explicit release gates, experiment explanations, commercial-pressure penalties, narrow-screen steppers, keyboard operation, rollback consequences, unlock pacing, and teardown across all six query sets.

queue.title: Fabline Control 1.1 strategy regression playtest
queue.reason: Verify scenario-specific recipes, control side effects, audited uncertainty, four release gates, contamination and drift pressure, throughput economics, narrow-screen steppers, keyboard operation, engineer recovery, unlock pacing, and teardown across all six lots.

queue.title: Collection Navigator stability regression playtest
queue.reason: Verify late-loading cards trigger one bounded refresh, sorting no longer self-schedules renders, shortlist and recent controls remain stable after reordering, keyboard shortcuts do not leak after teardown, and visible counts stay accurate.

queue.title: Folding Frontier 1.1 regression playtest
queue.reason: Verify numbered bond controls, collision rings, readiness messaging, hint highlighting, canvas selection, narrow-screen layout, keyboard rotation, target fairness, mentor recovery, and teardown across all six proteins.

queue.title: Privacy Ledger 1.1 strategy regression playtest
queue.reason: Verify scenario-specific control bonuses, control tradeoff explanations, audited and unaudited risk displays, readiness gates, budget composition, equity thresholds, disabled actions, narrow-screen controls, keyboard operation, counsel consequences, unlock pacing, and teardown across all six releases.

recentDecisions.title: Relevance Lab 1.1.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a Relevance Lab strategy pass, a crisis-negotiation simulator, and the open Ambient Ambient feedback proposal. Prioritized the queued search-ranking evidence gap because signals behaved too similarly, experiments exposed too little actionable evidence, and release readiness was hard to read. The release adds scenario-specific signal sensitivities, explicit signal tradeoffs, four visible release gates, blocker-aware experiment reports, clearer commercial-pressure penalties, improved disabled states, narrow-screen controls, and preserved local-only teardown without adding microphone permissions.
checks: No storage, permissions, secrets, or network additions

recentDecisions.title: Fabline Control 1.1.0
recentDecisions.summary: Compared a Fabline Control process-window pass, a crisis-negotiation simulator, and an Ambient Ambient prototype. Added scenario-specific recipes, audited yield ranges, release gates, maintenance consequences, and preserved local-only teardown.
